claims.text: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck at 5.5996, 125.0647 (offshore Sarangani, Philippines) on 7 June 2026 at 23:37 UTC, at 35 km depth, generating a maximum tsunami wave of 1.93 metres; GDACS issued an orange alert for medium humanitarian impact with about 2.9 million people exposed to Modified Mercalli Intensity VII or greater.

At least 19 people were killed, 12 remain missing and at least 134 were injured after the 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck Sarangani province on 8 June 2026, according to OCHA, with authorities noting the figures were provisional as assessments continued; more than 3.2 million learners were affected with classes suspended in over 6,200 schools.

By 23 June, government figures reported 77 deaths, with about 1.6 million people affected across Regions IX, XI, XII, and BARMM. PHIVOLCS recorded more than 9,400 aftershocks and further seismic activity expected. More than 80,000 houses have been damaged, including nearly 15,000 destroyed, with 884 damaged infrastructure losses and 130 affected roads and 47 bridges limiting access to isolated communities. Eighteen local government units (LGUs) have declared a state of calamity.

DSWD DROMIC Report #54, as of 07 July 2026, 6AM, reports 391,871 families (1,655,313 persons) affected in 650 barangays across Regions IX, XI, XII and BARMM, with 24,148 families (92,628 persons) still displaced — 7,059 families (28,462 persons) inside 74 evacuation centers and 17,089 families (64,166 persons) staying outside evacuation centers with relatives or friends. A total of 113,412 houses were damaged, of which 18,384 were totally destroyed and 95,028 partially damaged.

description: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck offshore Sarangani province in the southern Philippines on 8 June 2026 (7:37am local time / 7 June 23:37 UTC), triggering a tsunami of nearly 2 metres and widespread damage across Mindanao. GDACS issued an orange alert for medium humanitarian impact. The confirmed death toll and number of affected people climbed through late June as damage assessments continued, and eighteen local government units have declared a state of calamity.

impact.summary: As of 7 July 2026, DSWD DROMIC reported 1,655,313 people (391,871 families) affected across 650 barangays in Regions IX, XI, XII and BARMM, with 92,628 people still displaced. A total of 113,412 houses have been damaged, including 18,384 totally destroyed. Government figures last reported 77 deaths (as of 23 June). GDACS assessed an orange alert (medium humanitarian impact) with about 2.9 million people exposed to severe shaking and a tsunami of up to 1.93 metres; eighteen local government units have declared a state of calamity.

location.region: Offshore Sarangani province, Mindanao (near General Santos)

name: Mindanao M7.8 Earthquake & Tsunami — Philippines
